ideas.hook: This 3D-printed Royal Pop kit just launched
ideas.angle: Show the Cults3D wrist conversion kit side-by-side with our premium bundle, highlighting the missing tool, pouch, and finish. End with: 'Which one would you trust on your Royal Pop?'
ideas.cta: Comment WRIST and I’ll send the waitlist link
ideas.informed_by_trend: AP x Swatch Royal Pop wrist conversion kit launches
ideas.notes: Close-up of both kits on a Royal Pop, then side-by-side on wrist. Text overlay: 'Tool included? No / Yes'.

ideas.angle: Poll followers on preferred strap material for the Royal Pop wrist kit, using Wristbuddys’ strap collection as visual reference. Frame as: 'We’re finalizing the first colour—help us decide.'
ideas.cta: Vote now, then DM for early access
ideas.informed_by_trend: Royal Pop strap customization and material debates
ideas.notes: Use Wristbuddys’ strap images as poll background. Add sticker: 'DM for waitlist'.

ideas.hook: This is why most Royal Pop straps feel cheap
ideas.angle: Demonstrate how generic straps lack the adapter’s precision fit, showing our kit’s clip-in mechanism and silicone strap texture. Contrast with a loose strap on a Royal Pop.
ideas.cta: Join the early access list (link in bio)
ideas.informed_by_trend: Royal Pop strap ecosystem exploding: Helvetus, Wristbuddys, Wisstraps, Delugs
ideas.notes: Slow-motion clip of adapter snapping into place. Text overlay: 'No wobble. No gaps.'

ideas.hook: The Royal Pop deserves better than a random strap
ideas.angle: Caption announcing the waitlist: 'We’re building a premium wrist conversion kit—adapter, strap, tool, and pouch. No scratches. No wobble. Just a proper wristwatch. Early access opens soon.' Address the 'prefer lanyard/pocket' objection by emphasizing daily wearability.
ideas.notes: Use a flat-lay of the kit components on a neutral background. Caption tone: direct, premium.

ideas.angle: Show the adapter prototype clipping onto a Royal Pop, then shaking the watch to test security. Voiceover: 'If it’s going on the wrist, it can’t fall off. Here’s how we’re solving that.' Address the 'might scratch the watch' objection by showing the adapter’s soft-touch interior.
ideas.cta: Tell me what this should cost
ideas.notes: Close-up of adapter interior material. Text overlay: 'Scratch-free. Secure.'

summary: Today’s focus is leveraging competitor launches (Cults3D, Helvetus, Wristbuddys) to drive waitlist signups. Priority 1 contrasts our premium kit with a 3D-printed alternative, while polls and build content address objections and material preferences. All CTAs funnel to waitlist growth.
